items.description: South Devon coast from Start Point to Dawlish Warren
items.eaAreaName: Devon, Cornwall and the Isles of Scilly
items.message:
High tides, strong winds and large waves this afternoon may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible 3 hours either side of high tide on the afternoon of Wednesday and Thursday morning. 
High tides of 1.72mAOD at Teignmouth will be at 18:21 on Wednesday 29th increasing to 1.80 mAOD on Thursday morning. Strong Easterly Force 5 to Force 6 winds and offshore wave heights up to 2.6m are forecast. Recent storms have increased the risk of wave overtopping at Torcross. Hazardous conditions are forecast along Torcross seafront, with waves containing debris. 
Coastal conditions are expected to ease on Thursday evening. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Avoid beaches and coastal promenades, which may be flooded . 
This message will be updated by 3:00PM on 30 April 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.description: South Devon coast at Beesands, Torcross, Slapton and Dawlish
items.eaAreaName: Devon, Cornwall and the Isles of Scilly
items.message:
High tides, strong winds and large waves are expected to cause flooding tomorrow morning. 
Flooding is expected 3 hours either side of high tide on Thursday morning. 
High tides of 1.9 mAOD at Teignmouth will be at 06:37AM on Thursday. Strong Easterly Force 6 winds and offshore wave heights up to 2.6m are forecast. Recent storms have increased the risk of wave overtopping at Torcross. Hazardous conditions are forecast along Torcross seafront, with waves containing debris. Coastal conditions are expected to ease on Thursday evening. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Avoid beaches and coastal promenades, which may be flooded . 
This message will be updated by 3:00PM on 30 April 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.description: Groundwater flooding in the Great Shefford area
items.message:
Continued high groundwater levels may lead to flooding. Flooding of low-lying areas and roads is possible.

Groundwater levels are falling at our Northfield Farm borehole in the Great Shefford area. Groundwater levels are expected to fall slowly over the coming weeks in response to a period of more settled weather. Due to the nature of groundwater flooding, this situation could continue for several weeks or longer.

We are closely monitoring rainfall and groundwater levels.

Residents are advised to consider property resilience measures and ensure that pumps are working where they have been installed. Keep up to date with groundwater levels on the 'Get Flood Warnings' site on gov.uk.

This message will be updated by 4PM on the 5 May 2026, or as the situation changes.

items.description: Groundwater flooding in West Ilsley, East Ilsley, Compton, Chilton and West Hagbourne
items.message:
Groundwater levels are now falling steadily. We do not expect further flooding and the flood alert has been removed. 
Rivers and streams remain full due to high groundwater levels and will react rapidly to further rainfall. We do not expect further flooding and the flood alert has been removed. 
We will continue to monitor the situation and will issue further alerts or warnings if necessary.   
You are advised to take the opportunity to check any pumps are in good working order and that stocks of sandbags are replenished. Septic tanks may need emptying as soakaways may not work for some time.
